question.label: Self-read opener
question.name: Before I share my view, how do you think the last stretch has gone?
question.description: Anchors the conversation in their self-assessment first; gap vs manager's view becomes a signal.

answer: I can name wins, but I am not always sure they match the team bar.
note: He can identify wins but says he is not sure they meet the team bar, which is a strong clarity gap; willingness to name wins shows some engagement.

question.label: Thread follow
question.name: name wins, always — can you say more about what that means for you right now?
question.description: Runtime thread-follow injected because planner did not follow substantive answer.

answer: Feedback has helped, though I still miss consistency under time pressure.
note: He says feedback is helping, but inconsistency under time pressure still points to a mild unresolved clarity gap around what good looks like in execution.

question.label: Time pressure example
question.name: Under time pressure, where does the consistency slip show up most clearly?
question.description: Gets a concrete example of the quality inconsistency he named so the conversation can move into evidence.

answer: I sometimes focus on polish and lose sight of the core message.
note: He names a clear pattern: under pressure he prioritizes polish over the core message, which is a strong clarity gap in how he judges good work.

question.label: Core message miss
question.name: When you lose the core message, what are you missing in the brief?
question.description: Drills into the specific clarity failure he named before moving on.

answer: I want clearer examples of what excellent junior-level work looks like here.
note: He names a specific absence — clear examples of excellent junior-level work — which is a strong clarity gap; also hints at growth support being underprovided.

question.label: Gap awareness
question.name: Where do you think you've fallen short of where you want to be?
question.description: Tests whether they can name their own gaps before the manager does.

answer: When reviews are broad, I am not sure what to change first.
note: He names broad reviews leaving him unsure what to change first — a clear clarity gap with mild growth drag; no wellbeing signal.

question.label: Broad reviews
question.name: When reviews are broad, what specifically is missing for you?
question.description: Drills into the review pattern to name the exact gap in feedback specificity.

answer: I would like to grow faster in concept rationale, not just execution speed.
note: He names a specific growth gap — concept rationale over execution speed — which is a strong growth deficit signal, while the answer is clear and carries no wellbeing signal.

question.label: Concept rationale
question.name: Concept rationale — what makes that harder for you right now?
question.description: Drills into the specific growth gap he named to surface the underlying cause.

answer: A tighter loop on one skill at a time would help me improve.
note: Names a specific missing condition — tighter, single-skill feedback loops — which signals blocked growth and some lack of clarity on how to improve.

question.label: Improvement loop
question.name: What would a tighter one-skill feedback loop look like in practice?
question.description: Final cause-stage question to make the missing feedback structure concrete before closing on commitment.

answer: If we align on concrete criteria, I can work against them confidently.
note: He names concrete criteria alignment as the missing condition for confident execution, which is a positive clarity and growth signal; wellbeing was not engaged.

question.label: Practice commitment
question.name: What one habit do you want to practice before we meet again?
question.description: Closes on a concrete near-term behavior change with ownership.

answer: I will start by writing one explicit priority rule I will use when time pressure hits this month.
note: He named a specific self-directed practice for handling time pressure, which is a concrete positive commitment on growth and clarity.
